title: The Itchy Amp Scratchy Amp Poochie Show

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashes his car in the opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Homer's catchphrase - iconic to the character but not particularly creative here

jokes.text: I hope you enjoyed my one-man pie fight, kids.
jokes.setup: Krusty addresses kids after some unseen performance
jokes.punchline: The absurd concept of a 'one-man pie fight'
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist humor fitting Krusty's low-rent showbiz character

jokes.text: Now it's time for another 'fanschmabulous' episode
jokes.setup: Krusty introducing the cartoon
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'fanschmabulous' instead of 'fantastic'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay fitting Krusty's cheesy TV persona

jokes.text: But how can we watch TV when it's so beautiful out?
jokes.setup: Marge asking why they're not watching TV
jokes.punchline: Kids ironically choosing outdoors over television
jokes.explanation: Nice reversal of expected TV-obsessed kid behavior

jokes.text: Yeah, Mom. I mean, we love you and Dad too... but God knows we don't need to see you every day.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why they don't need to watch TV constantly
jokes.punchline: Casual admission they don't need to see their parents daily
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly crafted line that captures both childhood honesty and family dysfunction

jokes.text: An occasional hug is all I ask. / Mom! You can hug me when I'm asleep. / I do. / [Screams]
jokes.setup: Marge wanting affection, Bart saying she can hug him when asleep
jokes.punchline: Marge admitting she actually does, horrifying Bart
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect timing - goes from sweet to creepy in seconds

jokes.text: Yeah, it's a new kind of polymer treatment- Hey, shut up!
jokes.setup: Someone compliments Krusty's teeth
jokes.punchline: Krusty starts to explain then abruptly cuts himself off rudely
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's self-awareness and impatience

jokes.text: What happened here? Lightning hit the transmitter? / See, that's what I thought at first, but then- Hey, shut up!
jokes.setup: Roger starts to explain the ratings crater
jokes.punchline: Krusty cuts him off with the same 'Hey, shut up!' as before
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that reinforces Krusty's dismissive personality

jokes.text: That crater is where your lousy cartoon crash-landed! It's ratings poison!
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining the ratings chart crater
jokes.punchline: Treating the ratings drop as a literal crash landing crater
jokes.explanation: Creative visual metaphor mixing literal and figurative language

jokes.text: Acclaimed? [Spits] / I oughta replace it right now with that Chinese cartoon... where the robots turn into blingwads!
jokes.setup: Roger mentions critical acclaim for Itchy & Scratchy
jokes.punchline: Krusty's dismissal and threat to replace with nonsensical 'blingwads'
jokes.explanation: 'Blingwads' is perfectly nonsensical - sounds like real toy franchise while being complete gibberish

jokes.text: But I'm a lazy, lazy man, Roger.
jokes.setup: Krusty threatening to replace the show immediately
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of being too lazy to follow through
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Krusty's self-aware laziness is both honest and funny

jokes.text: And- I'll come back later.
jokes.setup: Mel enters to solicit donations mid-confrontation
jokes.punchline: Immediate recognition he's walked into a bad situation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective awkward timing gag

jokes.text: I need to purchase a brassiere. You kids wait over here in the credit department.
jokes.setup: Marge taking kids shopping
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about intimate apparel while parking kids in inappropriate location
jokes.explanation: Marge's awkward oversharing and poor parenting judgment

jokes.text: And be honest, because no one from the show is here spying on you.
jokes.setup: Focus group leader assuring kids of anonymity
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie given the two-way mirror setup
jokes.explanation: Standard focus group satire setup

jokes.text: Why is that mirror sneezing? / Uh, look, it's just an old, creaky mirror. You know, sometimes it sounds a little like it's sneezing... or coughing or talking softly.
jokes.setup: Ralph notices the two-way mirror observers
jokes.punchline: Leader's increasingly desperate and absurd explanations
jokes.explanation: Ralph's innocence exposing the obvious deception with escalating absurd cover story

jokes.text: My knob tastes funny. / Please refrain from tasting the knob.
jokes.setup: Kids given knobs to rate the show
jokes.punchline: Ralph doing the most Ralph thing possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum moment - innocent child doing inappropriate thing

jokes.text: One kid seems to love the Speedo man. What more do they want?
jokes.setup: Roger analyzing the focus group data
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting one kid's reaction to some random Speedo-wearing character
jokes.explanation: Absurd background detail that suggests bizarre content in the cartoon

jokes.text: So you want a realistic, down-to-earth show... that's completely off-the-wall and swarming with magic robots?
jokes.setup: Kids giving contradictory feedback about what they want
jokes.punchline: Leader highlighting the impossible contradictions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of focus group logic and network TV's impossible demands

jokes.text: And also you should win things by watching.
jokes.setup: Kids adding more contradictory demands
jokes.punchline: Completely unrelated demand for prizes
jokes.explanation: Classic kid logic - everything should have prizes

jokes.text: You kids don't know what you want. That's why you're still kids- 'cause you're stupid!
jokes.setup: Leader getting frustrated with contradictory feedback
jokes.punchline: Complete professional breakdown and insulting the children
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing adult losing it with kids in professional setting

jokes.text: It's as good as ever. But after so many years... the characters just can't have the same impact they once had.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining what's wrong with Itchy & Scratchy
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on The Simpsons itself and long-running shows
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary where Lisa diagnoses the show's own potential problems

jokes.text: Please sign these papers indicating that you did not save Itchy and Scratchy.
jokes.setup: Roger celebrating Lisa's insight
jokes.punchline: Immediately trying to steal credit through legal paperwork
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate credit-stealing and legal manipulation

jokes.text: Itchy and Scratchy comprise a dramaturgical dyad. / Hey, this ain't art! It's business!
jokes.setup: Writer using pretentious language about the show's structure
jokes.punchline: Executive's blunt dismissal of artistic considerations
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between writer pretension and business pragmatism

jokes.text: In your dreams. We're talking the original dog from hell. / You mean Cerberus?
jokes.setup: Executive describing a 'dog from hell'
jokes.punchline: Writer's literal interpretation vs. executive's metaphorical meaning
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between writer's education and executive's buzzword usage

jokes.text: You've heard the expression 'let's get busy.' Well, this is a dog who gets 'biz-zay.'
jokes.setup: Executive explaining the dog's attitude
jokes.punchline: Forced hip-hop spelling of 'busy' as character trait
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate attempts to sound hip and current

jokes.text: Excuse me, but 'proactive' and 'paradigm'- aren't these just buzzwords that dumb people use to sound important? / I'm fired, aren't I? / Oh, yes.
jokes.setup: Writer calling out corporate buzzwords
jokes.punchline: Immediate self-aware recognition of consequences and confirmation
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with perfect escalation and timing. Writer's honesty leading to instant karma

jokes.text: So, Poochie okay with everybody? / Yeah. / Yeah, you know, it's good.
jokes.setup: Executive asking for feedback on the terrible name
jokes.punchline: Writers' unenthusiastic, forced agreement
jokes.explanation: Good workplace cringe - everyone knows it's bad but won't say so

jokes.text: Oh, you know, attitude! Attitude! Uh, sunglasses!
jokes.setup: Executive being asked to define 'attitude'
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex character trait to simple accessory
jokes.explanation: Good satire of shallow corporate understanding of 'cool'

jokes.text: I feel we should 'rastafy' him by 10% or so.
jokes.setup: Committee trying to make Poochie cooler
jokes.punchline: Treating cultural appropriation as a precise percentage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire - treating cultural identity as a corporate slider setting

jokes.text: Adding a new character is often a desperate attempt to boost low ratings.
jokes.setup: Lisa seeing the Poochie announcement
jokes.punchline: Direct commentary on what the episode is literally doing
jokes.explanation: Pure meta-humor - Lisa explaining the exact TV trope the show is parodying

jokes.text: Yo, yo. How's it hangin', everybody? / Morning, Roy. / Yeah, hi, Roy.
jokes.setup: Random new character Roy appears in the Simpson house
jokes.punchline: Family casually acknowledging him like he's always been there
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - Roy IS the random character addition being parodied

jokes.text: You have a funny voice. / I do not! / Haven't you ever listened to yourself on a tape recorder? / I prefer to listen to Cheap Trick.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting Homer audition based on his voice
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive response and non-sequitur about music preference
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - defensive then randomly specific about irrelevant details

jokes.text: Hey, this is Homer Simpson saying- [Screams] / I don't sound like that, do I? / Oh, I don't like having such a hilarious voice.
jokes.setup: Homer hearing himself on tape recorder
jokes.punchline: Horror at his own voice then self-aware complaint
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-discovery moment with good escalation

jokes.text: Whoa! A talking dog. [Laughs] What were you guys smokin' when you came up with that?
jokes.setup: Homer at the audition reading about Poochie
jokes.punchline: Homer finding talking dog concept ridiculous in world of talking cartoon characters
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Homer mocking the concept while in The Simpsons universe

jokes.text: We were eating rotisserie chicken.
jokes.setup: Homer asking what drugs inspired talking dog
jokes.punchline: Completely mundane, literal answer
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan response that deflates Homer's drug joke assumption

jokes.text: Next to you, perfection is crap!
jokes.setup: Director praising Homer's audition
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously over-the-top comparison
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of praise to absurd levels

jokes.text: You may remember me from such cartoons as... Christmas Ape and Christmas Ape Goes to Summer Camp.
jokes.setup: Troy's signature introduction format
jokes.punchline: Absurd cartoon titles with Christmas Ape sequel
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure with perfectly absurd fake credits

jokes.text: Well, you can cram it with walnuts, ugly!
jokes.setup: Homer being told he has no attitude
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, specific insult that somehow demonstrates attitude
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird insult that's both specific and inexplicable - pure Homer creativity

jokes.text: Oh, now I got the job, huh? Oh, thank you.
jokes.setup: Getting hired because of his attitude outburst
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic gratitude that immediately deflates his anger
jokes.explanation: Good deflation moment - Homer's anger immediately turns to polite gratitude

jokes.text: She smells sheet smells by the sheet 'shtore.'
jokes.setup: Voice level check with tongue twister
jokes.punchline: Homer's characteristic pronunciation difficulties
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer speech impediment humor

jokes.text: You? But you're a lady. / [Itchy's Voice] She's a lady all right. / [Scratchy's Voice] A beautiful lady.
jokes.setup: Homer shocked that a woman voices male cartoon characters
jokes.punchline: June flirting with Homer using the character voices
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction with creative use of voice acting abilities

jokes.text: You mean 'meep-meep'? / No. They only paid me to say it once. Then they doubled it up on the soundtrack. Cheap bastards.
jokes.setup: Homer correcting June about Roadrunner sound
jokes.punchline: Revelation about cheap animation production practices
jokes.explanation: Brilliant behind-the-scenes satire that feels plausible and specific

jokes.text: No, Homer. Very few cartoons are broadcast live. It's a terrible strain on the animators' wrists.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if the episode is live
jokes.punchline: Treating animation like it could be live with specific physical concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic treating impossible scenario with mundane concerns

jokes.text: In episode 2F09, when Itchy plays Scratchy's skeleton like a xylophone... he strikes the same rib twice in succession... yet he produces two clearly different tones. I mean, what are we- to believe that this is some sort of a- [Chuckles] a magic xylophone or something?
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy asking overly detailed continuity question
jokes.punchline: Applying scientific logic to cartoon violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - pedantic overthinking of cartoon physics

jokes.text: Boy, I really hope somebody got fired for that blunder.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy's detailed continuity complaint
jokes.punchline: Demanding someone lose their job over cartoon physics
jokes.explanation: Escalation to absurd consequences for trivial cartoon inconsistency

jokes.text: Why would a man whose shirt says 'genius at work'... spend all of his time watching a children's cartoon show?
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Comic Book Guy's pedantry
jokes.punchline: Cutting straight to the real issue with the fan's priorities
jokes.explanation: Devastating comeback that perfectly skewers obsessive fandom culture

jokes.text: I withdraw my question.
jokes.setup: Homer's brutal comeback about Comic Book Guy's priorities
jokes.punchline: Immediate, defeated retreat from the argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflated response showing even Comic Book Guy has no comeback

jokes.text: is there a way to get out of the dungeon without using the wizard key? / What the hell are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Fan asking Homer about CD-ROM game mechanics
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete bewilderment at the specific question
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between fan obsession and Homer's cluelessness

jokes.text: Pardon me. Look out. Pardon me. Excuse me. Hot soup.
jokes.setup: Roy navigating through the crowd
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning he's carrying hot soup as if it explains everything
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor with Roy's random soup detail

jokes.text: 1969- man walks on the moon. 1971 - man walks on the moon... again. Then for a long time, nothing happened. Until tonight.
jokes.setup: Announcer building up TV history with moon landings
jokes.punchline: Equating Poochie's debut with moon landing importance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and satire of TV hype culture

jokes.text: Then for a long time, nothing happened.
jokes.setup: Listing significant TV moments after 1971
jokes.punchline: Dismissing decades of history as uneventful
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan dismissal of entire decades as boring

jokes.text: Ooh, Poochie is one outrageous dude. / He's totally in my face.
jokes.setup: Itchy and Scratchy forced to praise Poochie
jokes.punchline: Stilted, unnatural dialogue praising the new character
jokes.explanation: Good cringe factor from forced character integration

jokes.text: Wiggity, wiggity. Word up. Rock on, party.
jokes.setup: Poochie's debut appearance
jokes.punchline: String of disconnected 'cool' phrases that make no sense together
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of committee-designed 'cool' character failing

jokes.text: When are they gonna get to the fireworks factory? [Whining]
jokes.setup: Poochie dominating the cartoon screen time
jokes.punchline: Kid wanting actual Itchy & Scratchy violence instead
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on what audiences actually want vs. what they get

jokes.text: When are they gonna get to the fireworks factory? [Whining]
jokes.setup: Poochie's introduction scene dragging on
jokes.punchline: Kid asking about promised action that never comes
jokes.explanation: Perfect audience surrogate complaint about boring character development over action

jokes.text: Can somebody tell me what the hell is going on? Midge, help me out here.
jokes.setup: Grampa watching the confusing Poochie cartoon
jokes.punchline: Calling Marge 'Midge' while asking for help understanding
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa confusion with wrong name for Marge

jokes.text: Can somebody tell me what the hell is going on? Midge, help me out here.
jokes.setup: Confusing Poochie episode playing
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's confusion and wrong name for Marge
jokes.explanation: Standard Grandpa confusion with typical name mistake

jokes.text: Catch you on the flip side, 'dudemeisters.' Not!
jokes.setup: Poochie trying to be cool in his exit
jokes.punchline: Mixing multiple dated slang terms including 'Not!' joke format
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of trying too hard to be current and hip

jokes.text: Hey, kids, always recycle... to the extreme!
jokes.setup: Poochie delivering a moral message
jokes.punchline: Adding 'to the extreme' to mundane recycling advice
jokes.explanation: Good satire of forced environmental messages in kids' shows

jokes.text: Homer, I can honestly say that was the best episode of Impy and Chimpy I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Barney trying to compliment Homer on the show
jokes.punchline: Getting the cartoon name completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic Barney drunken confusion getting basic details wrong

jokes.text: Homer, I can honestly say that was the best episode of Impy and Chimpy I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Barney giving his opinion on the episode
jokes.punchline: Getting the show name completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Good Barney moment - supportive but clueless about basic details

jokes.text: Mom, can we go to bed without dinner? / Yes, we can.
jokes.setup: Kids wanting to escape the awkward situation
jokes.punchline: Marge allowing it, showing how bad the show was
jokes.explanation: Good measure of how bad the show was - kids prefer hunger to staying

jokes.text: [Thinking] Oh, you don't want to know what I really think.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Marge liked the show
jokes.punchline: Marge's internal diplomatic restraint
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's kindness vs. honesty conflict

jokes.text: Don't have a cow, Lise. / Bart's right. Let's none of us have a cow.
jokes.setup: Lisa's critique of buzzwords
jokes.punchline: Bart and Homer immediately using tired catchphrases
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic demonstration of Lisa's point happening immediately

jokes.text: Last night's Itchy and Scratchy was, without a doubt... the worst episode ever!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy's dramatic buildup
jokes.punchline: His signature hyperbolic criticism
jokes.explanation: Iconic Comic Book Guy line that became a meme - perfect character moment

jokes.text: What? They've given you thousands of hours of entertainment for free. What could they possibly owe you? I mean, if anything, you owe them.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy claiming the show owes him
jokes.punchline: Bart's logical counterargument about free entertainment
jokes.explanation: Smart commentary on fan entitlement culture from unexpected source

jokes.text: Worst episode ever.
jokes.setup: Bart's reasonable argument about free entertainment
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy completely ignoring the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect stubborn character consistency - won't engage with logic

jokes.text: Far be it from me to gloat in another's downfall... but I have a feeling no children are gonna be crying... when this puppy is put to sleep. [Laughing]
jokes.setup: Kent claiming not to gloat
jokes.punchline: Immediately gloating with dog death pun
jokes.explanation: Good hypocrisy and dark humor with the euthanasia pun

jokes.text: Well, I'd attribute the product failure... to fundamental shifts in our key demographic... coupled with the overall crumminess of Poochie.
jokes.setup: Executive analyzing the failure in corporate speak
jokes.punchline: Ending with blunt admission of quality
jokes.explanation: Good corporate buzzword buildup deflated by honest assessment

jokes.text: Uh, one: Poochie needs to be louder, angrier and have access to a time machine. Two: Whenever Poochie's not on screen... all the other characters should be asking, 'Where's Poochie?'
jokes.setup: Homer presenting ideas to save the show
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous demands culminating in meta-textual requirement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from simple to absurd to meta - becomes quotable meme

jokes.text: Two: Whenever Poochie's not on screen... all the other characters should be asking, 'Where's Poochie?'
jokes.setup: Homer's second improvement idea
jokes.punchline: Demanding constant attention for absent character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of vanity and character obsession that became a meme

jokes.text: Put a sock in it, Roy.
jokes.setup: Roy trying to support Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing the character addition he once represented
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Homer's hypocrisy about character additions

jokes.text: Put a sock in it, Roy.
jokes.setup: Roy trying to be supportive
jokes.punchline: Homer finally acknowledging Roy exists just to dismiss him
jokes.explanation: Great payoff - Homer finally directly addressing the obvious intruder

jokes.text: You're supposed to say, 'Please cut off my head. I don't deserve to live.'
jokes.setup: Homer refusing to follow the script
jokes.punchline: Revealing the extremely dark written dialogue
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing how thoroughly they planned to destroy Poochie

jokes.text: Never! / You can't just kill off a classic TV character. Poochie could be bigger than curly fries.
jokes.setup: Homer refusing to read the death script
jokes.punchline: Comparing cartoon character to fast food as measure of success
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - curly fries as the pinnacle of cultural success

jokes.text: I know there's a lot of people who don't like me and wish I would go away... and I think we got off on the wrong foot. I know I can come off a little proactive, and for that I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Poochie addressing the audience directly
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to apologize for being exactly what he was designed to be
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment where character apologizes for corporate mandated traits

jokes.text: I have to go now. My planet needs me.
jokes.setup: Poochie about to give his heartfelt speech
jokes.punchline: Sudden, completely different voice and explanation for departure
jokes.explanation: Perfect abrupt shift showing corporate override - became iconic meme

jokes.text: Wow! Poochie came from another planet? / Uh, I guess.
jokes.setup: Poochie's alien origin revelation
jokes.punchline: Kids' confused acceptance of nonsensical explanation
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to the absurd corporate fix

jokes.text: Hey, that wasn't supposed to happen! Those finks double-crossed me!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing his speech replaced
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's been betrayed by the network
jokes.explanation: Good Homer outrage at corporate manipulation

jokes.text: Well, kids, we all know that sometimes when cartoon characters die... they're back again the very next week. That's why I'm presenting this sworn affidavit... that Poochie will never, ever, ever return!
jokes.setup: Krusty acknowledging cartoon death conventions
jokes.punchline: Legal document to prevent cartoon resurrection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on cartoon conventions with legal absurdity

jokes.text: Good news, everybody. I'm movin' into my own apartment with two sexy ladies.
jokes.setup: Roy announcing his departure from the show
jokes.punchline: Giving him an off-screen life as he's written out
jokes.explanation: Nice completion of Roy's arc as he gets written out with implausible success

jokes.text: Well, I guess I learned my lesson. The thing is, I lost creative control of the project. And I forgot to ask for any money.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on his experience
jokes.punchline: Learning wrong lessons and revealing he worked for free
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ending - completely missing the point and revealing his incompetence

jokes.text: The thing is, I lost creative control of the project. And I forgot to ask for any money.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on the experience
jokes.punchline: Admitting to the two biggest mistakes in Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer lesson learned - focusing on the wrong details entirely

jokes.text: What else is on?
jokes.setup: Lisa praising the return to classic Itchy & Scratchy
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate boredom with the 'improved' show
jokes.explanation: Perfect cynical ending - even 'fixing' the show doesn't guarantee audience satisfaction

tmdb_overview: In an attempt to boost ratings, the Itchy and Scratchy Show adds a new "in-your-face" character voiced by Homer.
insight: Post-modern meta-comedy hits 84 jokes on Poochie's debut, deconstructing animation itself.
